name: Grade 8 Mathematics — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 8 pathway for linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 8 knowledge and transferable skills in linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 8 task connected to linear relationships, functions, exponents, and scientific notation.
